query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed the research. The full synthesis is in my report above, and structured findings have been submitted. Key takeaways:

- **Hichilema is the clear front-runner** for the 13 August 2026 election, with the one credible poll (Zambia Election Research Network, late 2025) putting him at **55%** — above the 50%+1 outright-win threshold, though it predates the main opposition campaign.
- **Edgar Lungu is not running: he died 5 June 2025**, and had already been barred by the Constitutional Court under the two-term limit. His death removed the opposition's key unifying figure and deepened PF factionalism.
- The **main challenger is Brian Mundubile** (Tonse–PF Pamodzi Alliance / NRPUP), a first-time candidate leaning on Lungu's legacy; the opposition is badly fragmented (26 filed, 14 approved, plus a Socialist Party bid and 184 independents).
- Hichilema's tailwinds: a recovering economy (4.3% projected GDP growth, 6.5% inflation, completed debt restructuring), incumbency, free education, and expanded CDF.
- **Turnout and voter apathy** — not opposition strength — are cited as Hichilema's biggest risks, with 8.7 million registered voters and lingering cost-of-living discontent despite macro gains.

Note: Zambian polling is thin and methodologically weak, so quantitative confidence is limited — the strongest evidence is qualitative analyst consensus that HH leads a fragmented, leaderless opposition.

sources.title: 2026 Zambian general election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: General elections are due to be held in Zambia on 13 August 2026 to elect a president, members of the national assembly, councillors and council chairs.

sources.title: Zambia election will put Hichilema's economic record to the test
sources.snippet: Hichilema favoured to win a second term; Zambia Election Research Network survey found 55% planned to vote for him; IMF projects 4.3% growth 2026; inflation 6.5% June 2026.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: Record 26 presidential candidates (14 approved); 8.7m registered voters; Cyber Crimes Act used against opposition; 70 new constituencies.

sources.title: Lungu's death and the perils of a weak opposition in Zambia
sources.snippet: Lungu died 5 June 2025; he was a polarising figure who unified the fragmented PF opposition.

sources.title: Edgar Lungu - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Edgar Chagwa Lungu (11 November 1956 - 5 June 2025), sixth president of Zambia; died in June 2025 following surgery complications in Pretoria.

sources.title: Zambia: Ex-president Lungu barred from seeking re-election
sources.snippet: Constitutional Court barred Lungu from contesting 2026 and future elections on two-term grounds.

sources.title: Candidate Profiles: Brian Mundubile - Open Zambia
sources.snippet: Mundubile is the Tonse-PF Pamodzi Alliance candidate on an NRPUP ticket, running mate Makebi Zulu; PF deeply factionalized after Lungu.

sources.title: Can Zambia's fragmented opposition unseat 'Mighty' Hichilema on August 13 polls?
sources.snippet: 26 candidates cleared; Hichilema the favorite; opposition split across Tonse Alliance and People's Pact; 8.7m registered voters.

sources.title: 2026 Election Poll Survey Analysis - Zambia Streets
sources.snippet: Provincial vote-share estimates (UPND Southern 74%, NRPUP Luapula 76%); no firm, sample size or dates given - low reliability.

sources.title: The Biggest Opposition to Hichilema May Be Voter Apathy
sources.snippet: Argues voter apathy, not opposition parties, is Hichilema/UPND's greatest challenge in 2026.

sources.title: Zambia's 2026 election: How Hichilema is tilting the playing field
sources.snippet: Sishuwa Sishuwa alleges electoral irregularities and playing-field tilt ahead of 13 August 2026 vote.

sources.title: Zambia locks in 8.7 million voters ahead of August election
sources.snippet: 8.7 million registered voters confirmed for the August 2026 election.

sources.title: Zambia: The Challenges of Maintaining Democratic Progress - Africa Center
sources.snippet: Hichilema seeking second term; won 59% in 2021 galvanized by youth turnout; population ~22 million.
